clinicalProblem.clinicalIntent: Prevent or treat elevated blood pressure and hypertension through dietary sodium restriction
clinicalProblem.whenToUse: Initial prevention counseling, hypertension diagnosis, BP management visit, dietary assessment
clinicalProblem.decisionOrAction: Sodium reduction recommendation with daily intake targets + contraindication screening

navigationPath.purpose: Entry point for dietary sodium assessment
Screen for severe symptomatic orthostatic hypotension
Estimate patient's current daily sodium intake
Set 2300 mg/d target with 1500 mg/d ideal goal
Provide sodium reduction strategies and food sources

validationRules: If orthostaticHypotensionSymptoms == true AND severity == 'severe' AND symptomaticStatus == true → contraindication

rules: Check for contraindication: severe, symptomatic orthostatic hypotension
If contraindication present → do not recommend sodium reduction
If no contraindication → recommend reduction to <2300 mg/d
Provide ideal goal of <1500 mg/d for optimal benefit
Applicable to adults with OR without hypertension
Recommendation strength: Class 1 (STRONG), Level of Evidence: A

categories.name: Class Assignment
categories.quality: High-quality evidence from >1 RCT or meta-analyses

escalationLogic.ifAnySymptomPresent: Contraindicate sodium reduction, evaluate for underlying cause of orthostatic hypotension

appDisplay.screenType: Standard clinical recommendation with contraindication alert

nextStepRouting.providerSuggestion: Implement dietary sodium reduction to <2300 mg/d with ideal goal <1500 mg/d
nextStepRouting.followUpTiming: Reassess sodium intake and BP in 4-8 weeks
nextStepRouting.repeatMeasurement: Monitor 24-hour urinary sodium excretion if available
nextStepRouting.navigatorEscalation: Flag if patient has severe symptomatic orthostatic hypotension
nextStepRouting.documentation: Log Class 1, LOE A recommendation to health record with target goals
